title: Attention as the Sole Connection
concept: By using self-attention, the model connects all positions directly, reducing path length between distant tokens to a single operation.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using self-attention to connect tokens?

options.text: Connect all words directly in a single step

exercises.explain: Self-attention is designed to link words across a sentence instantly, regardless of how far apart they are.

cards.title: Instant Teleportation
cards.body: In long sentences, early words often get forgotten by the time a model reaches the end. What if we could teleport information across any distance instantly? That is the superpower of self-attention.

cards.title: Direct Connections
cards.body: Self-attention acts as the sole connection mechanism in Transformers. Instead of passing information step-by-step through a chain, it allows every single word in a sentence to look at and connect with every other word directly in one quick operation.

cards.title: The Walkie-Talkie Classroom
cards.body: Imagine a classroom playing the game of 'Telephone' to pass a secret. In a traditional line (RNN), the message travels student by student, getting weaker at the end. But with self-attention, every student has a direct walkie-talkie to every other student. Distance doesn't matter; they can connect instantly in a single call.

cards.title: Bridging the Gap
cards.body:
In the sentence: 'The dog, which had chased the cat up the tree earlier in the afternoon, barked.'
An older model has to carry the word 'dog' through twelve steps to connect it to 'barked'. Self-attention connects 'dog' directly to 'barked' in a single step, ignoring all the words in between.

cards.title: The Distance Myth
cards.body: You might think that connecting every word to every other word requires a complicated chain of intermediate steps. In reality, self-attention does this in a single mathematical step, making the path length between any two words exactly one!

cards.title: Chain vs. Web Connection
cards.body: Let's compare how traditional models and self-attention models connect distant words in a sentence.

right.text: All words are connected by direct paths. The distance between any two words is always exactly one operation.

left.text: Information travels step-by-step down a chain. Distant words have a long path to travel, losing strength along the way.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of using self-attention as the sole connector in a model?

options.text: It reduces the path length between any two words to a single operation.
It forces the model to read the sentence backwards.
It increases the number of steps needed to connect words.
It deletes middle words to make sentences shorter.

exercises.explain: By connecting all positions directly, self-attention keeps the path length between any two tokens at exactly one operation, preventing information from getting lost.

exercises.prompt: True or False: With self-attention, the path length between the first word and the last word of a book is the same as the path length between two adjacent words.
exercises.explain: This is true! Self-attention connects every position to every other position directly, meaning the distance between them is always a single step.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about path length in self-attention.
exercises.sentence: No matter how far apart two words are in a sentence, self-attention connects them in exactly [blank] step.
exercises.explain: Because self-attention connects all positions directly, the distance or path length between any two words is always exactly one operation.

exercises.prompt: Which model demonstrates self-attention acting as the direct connection between distant words?

options.text: Model A processes 'The' -> then 'dog' -> then 'barked' in a slow sequence.
Model B instantly links 'The' directly to 'barked' across a 10-word gap.
Model C reads the words 'The' and 'barked' but keeps them completely separated.

exercises.explain: The option showing a direct link between the first and last words represents self-attention's ability to create immediate connections, while the others represent step-by-step or isolated processing.

exercises.prompt: How does self-attention solve the problem of long-distance connections in a sentence compared to older models?

options.text: It processes words one by one, making the path longer for later words.
It connects all word pairs directly, keeping the path length to exactly one step.
It ignores distant words and only connects neighboring words.
It doubles the path length between words to improve accuracy.

exercises.explain: Self-attention allows the Transformer to link any two words in a single step, bypassing the step-by-step traversal required by older architectures. This keeps the information path length constant at 1.
